MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. Issues of fact cannot be properly addressed on this motion. While Deutsche Bank, as trustee, is mentioned in the SEC filing dated July 26, 2006 along with WaMu as seller and servicer, the operative Assignment is dated May 26, 2006 and bears the notation "Chase Assignment" on the first page along with reference to Select Portfolio as the servicer of the second page. The veracity, validity and legal effect of each of the documents and writings therein are issues of fact that cannot be addressed on the pleadings. The Court notes that the moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (no courtesy copy of the operative complaint provided) and opposing party failed to comply wit SFLR 2.7B (no courtesy copy of opposition provided). In the future such failures will result in motions being taken off calendar/treated as unopposed. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Andrew Martin, Esq., 415-755-5599, appearing for Beatrice Gise, Plaintiff.
Nicholas Karkazis, Esq., 916-447-0700, appearing for Deutsche Bank, Defendant

Argued. Mr. Martin stated that he contested the tentative ruling and argued that the wrongful foreclosure accured on July 26, 2018.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to delayed discovery. Plaintiff's causes of action arise solely out of purportedly fraudulent recordation of the Assignment on May 19, 2014. The statute of limitations for all causes of action rooted in this recordation started on that date, unless Plaintiff can in good faith allege delayed discovery. Plaintiff's theory that the triggering event for the statute of limitations is a review of the documents by counsel is not supported by any authority. Plaintiff shall allege in good faith facts showing that her discovery of the Assignment occurred within 3 years of September 24, 2019. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
